Start sir.
This is a patient of a metastatic adenocarcinoma with malignant pleural effusion.
We have done a thoracoscopic pleural biopsy.
And now we are planning to insert an indwelling pleural catheter because the underlying lung is not expanded.
We have already done a thoracoscopy through this port.
So now we will put the small incision.
Start it up.
This is a tunneller which is provided by the company.
You can use this or any other instrument as required.
Dilator.
Because this is, turcoscopy has already been done for this patient, this is easy.
So once the tunneling is done, you have to pull out the catheter like this and make sure this is the teflon cuff.
That cuff has to lie under the skin here.
So once the cuff is lying under the skin, take out the dilator.
Don't peel it before you start inserting the catheter.
Once you are sure the catheter is inside, then start peeling it.
And as you peel it, push the catheter inside so that it goes inside the subcutaneous tunnel and into the chest.
Then you will be closing this wound with a silk stitch and then this catheter can be fixed to the skin with a silk stitch.
Thank you.
